episode_summary: A Little Fish episode with Andrew Hunter Murray, Dan Schreiber, and Anna Ptaszynski shares listener-submitted curiosities (from Montana’s “reasonable and prudent” speed signs to nuclear-pool cleaning robots, toucan beaks, early Guildford “baseball,” and more) before assigning classic headline facts to Club Fish members. The classic facts covered pre-corridor train ticket checks, Kaiser Wilhelm losing an arms deal for a bottom-slap, the uncertainty over pronouncing Louis/Louie Armstrong, and Iceland’s Althing parliament.

facts.fact: And the fact is, before trains had corridors, guards had to climb along the outside of the carriage to check your ticket.
And the fact is that Kaiser Wilhelm, once lost a valuable arms contract for Germany because he slapped the king of Bulgaria on the bottom.
Ryan Jackson, your fact is now that no one is quite sure how to say Louis Armstrong or Louis Armstrong.
The first thing was a parliament. And it was the old thing in Iceland.
